title: leria nervosa
parts: Leria nervosa has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of the plant contain medicinal constituents like alkaloids and glycosides, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the Leria nervosa plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of the plant contain volatile oils and terpenes, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
side_effects: Leria nervosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach cramps in some individuals. Prolonged or excessive use of this plant can also lead to dehydration, electrolyte imbalances, and kidney damage. In severe cases, it may cause seizures, coma, and even death due to the plant's toxic compounds interacting with the body's systems. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Leria nervosa for any medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
